full: Physical similarity can model the collision, but thick description is needed to model the accident as accusation, negligence, testimony, injury, or injustice. One event supports multiple models because different institutions ask different questions of it.

kernel: There are symbols in science as surely as in poetry.
full: There are symbols in science as surely as in poetry. The difference is not: <Poetry> = symbolic <Science> = literal. The stronger distinction is: <Poetry> organizes appearances and possible meanings. <Science> organizes testable relations through formalized symbols. <Religion> organizes conceptions and dispositions under a claim of ultimate reality.

kernel: <Symbolic_Form> → <Conception> → <Interpretive_Practice> → <Disposition> → <Action> → <Experienced_Confirmation> → <Reinforced_Symbolic_Order>.
full: <Symbolic_Form> → <Conception> → <Interpretive_Practice> → <Disposition> → <Action> → <Experienced_Confirmation> → <Reinforced_Symbolic_Order>.

kernel: <Model_Of> represents how some domain is understood.
full: <Model_Of> represents how some domain is understood. <Model_For> directs how a domain should be produced, inhabited, or transformed. A religious cosmology is both. So is an architectural plan, legal code, scientific simulation, economic forecast, or AI classifier.

kernel: Science generally asks: <What_Relations_Hold?> <What_Would_Disconfirm_Them?> <What_Follows_Under_Specified_Conditions?> Religion generally asks…
full: Science generally asks: <What_Relations_Hold?> <What_Would_Disconfirm_Them?> <What_Follows_Under_Specified_Conditions?> Religion generally asks: <What_Is_Ultimately_Real?> <How_Should_One_Live_In_Light_Of_It?> <How_Can_Suffering_And_Disorder_Be_Endured_Or_Interpreted?> Both questions are symbolically mediated.

kernel: A world model is never merely a model of the world once institutions act through it.
full: A world model is never merely a model of the world once institutions act through it. It becomes a model for the world: <Prediction> → <Decision> → <Intervention> → <Changed_Reality> → <New_Training_Data>. Representation becomes governance.

kernel: An AI-generated risk score, emotion label, behavioral forecast, or synthetic environment acquires an “aura of factuality” when embedded in institutional rituals…
full: An AI-generated risk score, emotion label, behavioral forecast, or synthetic environment acquires an “aura of factuality” when embedded in institutional rituals: <Data_Collection> → <Model_Output> → <Professional_Dashboard> → <Official_Decision> → <Material_Consequence>. The interface can make a contested interpretation appear as an observed fact.

kernel: Wittgensteinian models require correlations that let a representation touch reality.
full: Wittgensteinian models require correlations that let a representation touch reality. Geertz adds that public symbolic systems determine why those correlations matter, how they are interpreted, and which actions they authorize. <Model> + <Cultural_Use> + <Institutional_Authority> → <Social_Reality>.

kernel: Film can operate as both model of and model for experience.
full: Film can operate as both model of and model for experience. It shows a world while training viewers in how to recognize danger, intimacy, heroism, normality, causation, and emotional resolution.

kernel: The paper provides evidence that language models can become effective learned emulators of bounded symbolic environments when trained directly on action-conditioned transition histories.
full: The paper provides evidence that language models can become effective learned emulators of bounded symbolic environments when trained directly on action-conditioned transition histories. That is a significant result, but it is narrower than showing that language models possess general internal models of the physical or social world.

kernel: <Real_Interaction_Trajectories> → <Action_State_Dialogues> → <Dynamics_Aligned_Supervised_Training> → <History_Conditioned_Transition_Model> → <Predicted_Observation_And_Reward> → <Agent_Action> → <Extended_Synthetic_Trajectory>.
full: <Real_Interaction_Trajectories> → <Action_State_Dialogues> → <Dynamics_Aligned_Supervised_Training> → <History_Conditioned_Transition_Model> → <Predicted_Observation_And_Reward> → <Agent_Action> → <Extended_Synthetic_Trajectory>.

kernel: <Text_Continuation_Model> predicts likely language.
full: <Text_Continuation_Model> predicts likely language. <Protocol_Emulator> reproduces responses under a fixed interaction format. <State_Tracking_Emulator> maintains relevant variables across turns. <Counterfactual_Model> correctly distinguishes outcomes under alternative actions. <Interactive_Simulator> sustains closed-loop trajectories across diverse policies. <General_World_Model> transfers its causal structure across environments, interfaces, and embodiments. The paper strongly supports the middle categories in structured text domains.

kernel: <Level_1> One-step textual fidelity.
full: <Level_1> One-step textual fidelity. <Level_2> Long-horizon state consistency. <Level_3> Robustness under unfamiliar agents and environments. <Level_4> Correctly different consequences for controlled alternative actions. <Level_5> Resistance to simulator exploitation. <Level_6> Transfer of imagined policies into independently grounded environments. <Level_7> Measurable downstream agent utility.

kernel: The strongest evidence of world modeling would take the form: <Same_History> + <Action_A> → <Correct_State_A> and <Same_History> + <Action_B> → <Correctly_Different_State_B>.
full: The strongest evidence of world modeling would take the form: <Same_History> + <Action_A> → <Correct_State_A> and <Same_History> + <Action_B> → <Correctly_Different_State_B>. Prediction of ordinary next states is weaker evidence than accurate intervention-sensitive divergence.

kernel: The textual environment is a cultural program: it defines valid entities, permissible actions, success, failure, and which state changes count as meaningful.
full: The textual environment is a cultural program: it defines valid entities, permissible actions, success, failure, and which state changes count as meaningful. The model learns both a model of this world and a model for acting within it.

kernel: The textual state does not resemble the complete underlying environment.
full: The textual state does not resemble the complete underlying environment. It represents it because the protocol establishes operational correspondences between: <Textual_Signs> <Environment_States> <Permitted_Actions> <Resulting_Transitions>. The interface is a calibrated model, not a transparent copy.

kernel: Once the simulator verifies actions and manufactures training experience, it governs which futures the agent considers plausible, which risks it avoids, and which behaviors it rehearses.
full: Once the simulator verifies actions and manufactures training experience, it governs which futures the agent considers plausible, which risks it avoids, and which behaviors it rehearses. A world model becomes a constitutional layer for the policy.

kernel: An idea is not fully understood by defining what it represents.
full: An idea is not fully understood by defining what it represents. It must be translated into a conditional program: <Given_Conditions> + <Proposed_Operation> → <Expected_Consequences>. Its meaning becomes clearer through this projected difference, and its validity becomes clearer when the difference is tested.

kernel: <Indeterminate_Situation> → <Selective_Description> → <Hypothesis> → <Projected_Consequences> → <Experimental_Action> → <Observed_Consequences> → <Situation_Reconstructed_Or_Hypothesis_Revised>.
full: <Indeterminate_Situation> → <Selective_Description> → <Hypothesis> → <Projected_Consequences> → <Experimental_Action> → <Observed_Consequences> → <Situation_Reconstructed_Or_Hypothesis_Revised>.

kernel: <Peirce> asks what general habits of conduct a concept implies.
full: <Peirce> asks what general habits of conduct a concept implies. <James> asks what concrete experiential difference adopting a belief makes. <Dewey> asks how concepts function as instruments for transforming problematic situations.

kernel: <Meaning> concerns the conceivable consequences of using a concept.
full: <Meaning> concerns the conceivable consequences of using a concept. <Truth> concerns whether the projected consequences survive inquiry and experience. <Value> concerns whether the resulting transformation creates a more desirable situation. These should not be collapsed into one criterion.

kernel: A world model is pragmatically meaningful only if it supports counterfactual action: <Current_State> + <Possible_Action> → <Predicted_Consequence>.
full: A world model is pragmatically meaningful only if it supports counterfactual action: <Current_State> + <Possible_Action> → <Predicted_Consequence>. Its value is not exhausted by producing plausible descriptions. It must help an agent anticipate, test, and revise its conduct.

kernel: Sterrett’s model touches reality through correlations and preserved transformations.
full: Sterrett’s model touches reality through correlations and preserved transformations. Pragmatism adds the experimental criterion: A model’s meaning lies in the consequences its use licenses, and its truth is tested by whether those consequences survive contact with the modeled situation.

kernel: Geertz’s symbols are models of and models for reality.
full: Geertz’s symbols are models of and models for reality. Dewey explains the transition between them: <Symbolic_Conception> → <Guided_Action> → <Changed_Experience> → <Revised_Conception>. Cultural symbols do not merely represent worlds; they participate in making them.

kernel: A cinematic image is not only a representation to be interpreted.
full: A cinematic image is not only a representation to be interpreted. It can be an instrument that reorganizes perception, expectation, emotion, and future action. The pragmatic question is: <What_Difference_Does_This_Image_Make_In_Subsequent_Experience?>

kernel: Youngblood’s feedback loop becomes pragmatist when machine-generated variation is evaluated through its consequences for subsequent creation: <Proposal> → <Perceptual_Experience> → <Judgment> → <Revised_Operation>.
full: Youngblood’s feedback loop becomes pragmatist when machine-generated variation is evaluated through its consequences for subsequent creation: <Proposal> → <Perceptual_Experience> → <Judgment> → <Revised_Operation>. The system becomes intelligent not by generating endlessly, but by supporting consequential revision.

kernel: Cinema is not primarily a machine for recording objects.
full: Cinema is not primarily a machine for recording objects. It is a machine for differentiating functions within a field of change: <Perceive> <Be_Affected> <Act> <Relate> <Remember> <Expose_Time>.

kernel: <Set_Or_Frame> selects a relatively closed collection of elements.
full: <Set_Or_Frame> selects a relatively closed collection of elements. <Shot_Or_Movement> transforms the relations among those elements. <Whole_Or_Open> changes qualitatively as movements connect with what exceeds the frame. The shot is therefore not merely content inside a rectangle. It mediates between a selected system and an open whole undergoing change.

kernel: <Acentred_Field_Of_Movements> → <Interval> → <Selective_Perception> → <Intensive_Affection> → <Possible_Action> → <Changed_Milieu> → <New_Perception>.
full: <Acentred_Field_Of_Movements> → <Interval> → <Selective_Perception> → <Intensive_Affection> → <Possible_Action> → <Changed_Milieu> → <New_Perception>.

kernel: <Perception_Image> selects what matters for possible action.
full: <Perception_Image> selects what matters for possible action. <Affection_Image> holds intensive potential between stimulus and response. <Impulse_Image> expresses drives operating between originary worlds and derived milieus. <Action_Image> actualizes forces through situated behaviour. <Mental_Image> makes relations, interpretations, laws, and signs themselves into the object of cinema.

kernel: Whitney asks: <How_Shall_Motion_Pattern_Time?> Deleuze asks: <What_Function_Does_Movement_Perform_As_An_Image?> Whitney supplies a compositional theory of differential rate, resonance, tension, and resolution.
full: Whitney asks: <How_Shall_Motion_Pattern_Time?> Deleuze asks: <What_Function_Does_Movement_Perform_As_An_Image?> Whitney supplies a compositional theory of differential rate, resonance, tension, and resolution. Deleuze supplies an ontological-semiotic theory of perception, affection, action, relation, and time. Together they yield: <Temporal_Pattern> + <Image_Function> = <Cinematic_Thought>.

kernel: Knowlton provides executable visual operations.
full: Knowlton provides executable visual operations. Deleuze provides the image-regimes those operations can instantiate. A line shift might function as: <Perception> when it reveals a field. <Affection> when it becomes pure intensity. <Action> when it modifies a situation. <Relation> when it makes a rule or connection visible. The command does not determine the cinematic sign; its role within the sensory-motor structure does.

kernel: Deleuze explains how a body selects, feels, and acts within a milieu.
full: Deleuze explains how a body selects, feels, and acts within a milieu. Geertz asks what those actions mean within public symbolic systems. A complete cultural cinema model therefore requires: <Physical_Movement> → <Perceptual_Selection> → <Affective_Intensity> → <Situated_Action> → <Public_Interpretation> → <Institutional_Consequence>.

kernel: The classical action-image resembles pragmatic inquiry: <Problematic_Situation> → <Perception> → <Hypothesis_Or_Response> → <Action> → <Transformed_Situation>.
full: The classical action-image resembles pragmatic inquiry: <Problematic_Situation> → <Perception> → <Hypothesis_Or_Response> → <Action> → <Transformed_Situation>. Deleuze contributes the failure case pragmatism often backgrounds: situations in which perception is clichéd, action is impossible, consequences are disconnected, and thought begins only because the sensory-motor circuit breaks.

kernel: Most contemporary world models are implicitly action-image machines.
full: Most contemporary world models are implicitly action-image machines. They seek to predict: <Observation> + <Action> → <Next_State>. Deleuze shows that this is only one regime of images. A cinematic world model must also represent: <Selective_Perception> <Unresolved_Affect> <Impulse> <Ambiguous_Relation> <Failure_Of_Action> <Direct_Duration>.

kernel: A branch should not be defined only as a different event path.
full: A branch should not be defined only as a different event path. It can alter the image-regime itself: <Perception_Branch> changes what can be noticed. <Affection_Branch> changes which potentials intensify. <Action_Branch> changes what is done. <Relation_Branch> changes how events are interpreted. <Time_Branch> suspends action and exposes duration directly.

kernel: To direct is not merely to choose content or camera position.
full: To direct is not merely to choose content or camera position. It is to determine what kind of image a movement becomes and how one regime passes into another. [direct] = [shape] the passage among {<Perception>, <Affection>, <Impulse>, <Action>, <Relation>, <Time>}.

kernel: The file is assembling a textual control theory for generative media: <Thick_Prompt> establishes the meaningful conditions of a world.
full: The file is assembling a textual control theory for generative media: <Thick_Prompt> establishes the meaningful conditions of a world. <Generative_Model> renders or evolves that world. <Operative_Description> externalizes the model’s current state into an editable specification. <Revision> changes the world through textual intervention.

kernel: Thick prompting is the specification of the contextual relations that make generated events meaningful and consequential.
full: Thick prompting is the specification of the contextual relations that make generated events meaningful and consequential. It supplies not simply: <Objects> <Appearance> <Motion>, but: <Intentions> <Social_Codes> <Roles> <Histories> <Institutional_Conditions> <Stakes> <Expected_Consequences> <Competing_Interpretations>.

kernel: Operative description is a machine-actionable account of a generated artifact or world state whose terms can be edited to produce controlled transformations in that artifact or its subsequent evolution.
full: Operative description is a machine-actionable account of a generated artifact or world state whose terms can be edited to produce controlled transformations in that artifact or its subsequent evolution. Its value depends upon whether the description preserves: <Entity_Identity> <State> <Relations> <Constraints> <Causal_Dependencies> <Permitted_Operations> <Transformation_History>.

kernel: <Caption> says what an artifact appears to contain.
full: <Caption> says what an artifact appears to contain. <Prompt> requests an artifact. <Specification> states the conditions the artifact must satisfy. <Operative_Description> exposes those conditions after or during generation so they can be revised and reapplied.

kernel: The generative imagetext should not be identified with a hidden embedding alone.
full: The generative imagetext should not be identified with a hidden embedding alone. It is better understood as the complete operational relation among: <Textual_Instruction> <Visual_Output> <Model_State> <Editing_History> <Interpretive_Context> <Available_Transformations>.

kernel: Once the generated artifact is treated as a world, thick prompting must specify more than appearance: <What_Exists> <What_Persists> <What_Entities_Know> <What_Actions_Mean> <What_Actions_Change> <What_Rules_Cannot_Be_Violated> <What_Remains_Contested>.
full: Once the generated artifact is treated as a world, thick prompting must specify more than appearance: <What_Exists> <What_Persists> <What_Entities_Know> <What_Actions_Mean> <What_Actions_Change> <What_Rules_Cannot_Be_Violated> <What_Remains_Contested>.

kernel: A thick prompt should not force one interpretation to become unquestionable fact.
full: A thick prompt should not force one interpretation to become unquestionable fact. It should be capable of preserving: <Actor_Interpretation> <Observer_Interpretation> <Institutional_Interpretation> <Counter_Interpretation>. Otherwise contextual richness becomes semantic authoritarianism.

kernel: Operative ekphrasis gives language productive force: text causes an image to appear.
full: Operative ekphrasis gives language productive force: text causes an image to appear. Operative description adds a persistent control requirement: the resulting image or world must return an editable textual account capable of directing its next transformation. `Operative ekphrasis the collapse of the text image distinction in multimodal AI.pdf`

kernel: The project requires a layered ontology rather than one analogy: <Surface> what is visibly or verbally rendered.
full: The project requires a layered ontology rather than one analogy: <Surface> what is visibly or verbally rendered. <State> what currently exists in the represented domain. <Dynamics> how state may change. <Action> what interventions are available. <Constraint> what must remain invariant. <Simulation> the execution of state transitions. <World_Model> the learned mechanism that predicts those transitions. <World_Text> the symbolic specification through which those entities, relations, actions, and transition constraints become expressible and editable.

kernel: <Image> a perceptible visual configuration.
full: <Image> a perceptible visual configuration. <Image_Model> a generative distribution over visual configurations. <Video> a visible temporal sequence. <Video_Model> a generative distribution over temporal visual sequences. <World> a domain of persistent entities, relations, affordances, rules, and consequences. <World_Model> a predictive model of how that domain changes under endogenous and exogenous interventions. <World_Text> a symbolic, interpretable, and executable specification of that domain and its possible transformations.

kernel: A <Video_Model> answers: <What_Visible_Sequence_Is_Likely_To_Follow?> A <World_Model> answers: <What_State_Follows_If_This_Action_Is_Taken?> A <World_Text> must answer…
full: A <Video_Model> answers: <What_Visible_Sequence_Is_Likely_To_Follow?> A <World_Model> answers: <What_State_Follows_If_This_Action_Is_Taken?> A <World_Text> must answer: <What_Exists?> <What_Relations_Hold?> <What_Actions_Are_Possible?> <What_Changes?> <What_Must_Persist?> <What_Remains_Underdetermined?>

kernel: A future definition of <World_Text> should preserve the lesson of <Imagetext>: the symbolic and simulated layers are not independent channels later connected.
full: A future definition of <World_Text> should preserve the lesson of <Imagetext>: the symbolic and simulated layers are not independent channels later connected. They co-produce what the world can be perceived as, acted upon as, and interpreted as.

kernel: A future definition of <World_Text> must also preserve the operational structure of a world model: <Current_State> + <Action> → <Predicted_Next_State>.
full: A future definition of <World_Text> must also preserve the operational structure of a world model: <Current_State> + <Action> → <Predicted_Next_State>. Without transition logic, it remains descriptive text rather than world-text.

kernel: A world-text cannot contain only objects and physics.
full: A world-text cannot contain only objects and physics. It must also encode or expose: <Actor_Roles> <Intentions> <Social_Codes> <Institutional_Rules> <Competing_Interpretations> <Consequences_Of_Classification>. Otherwise it models motion without modeling meaning.

kernel: The meaning of a world-text lies in what transformations it enables and what consequences follow from using it.
full: The meaning of a world-text lies in what transformations it enables and what consequences follow from using it. A valid world-text must therefore be testable through intervention, not merely readable as description.

kernel: A world-text should not reduce cinema to action-conditioned state prediction.
full: A world-text should not reduce cinema to action-conditioned state prediction. It must also be capable of specifying: <Perception> <Affection> <Impulse> <Action> <Relation> <Crisis_Of_Action> <Duration>. A world is not only what changes under action; it is also what becomes perceptible when action fails.

kernel: The project should not define <Worldtext> as text that describes a world or even text that generates one.
full: The project should not define <Worldtext> as text that describes a world or even text that generates one. It should define the term around persistent operational binding: <Symbolic_Expression> ↔ <World_State> ↔ <Permitted_Transition> ↔ <Rendered_Experience>.

kernel: <World_Description> human-readable account of entities, situations, meanings, and stakes.
full: <World_Description> human-readable account of entities, situations, meanings, and stakes. <World_Specification> machine-interpretable state, relations, constraints, affordances, and transition conditions. <World_Model> learned or explicit mechanism predicting state change. <Simulator> process that executes transitions through time. <Renderer> process that converts state into image, video, sound, or text. <Worldtext> the editable symbolic layer that binds description, specification, and evolving simulated state.

kernel: The useful relation is not: <Image> : <Image_Model> as <Video> : <World_Model>.
full: The useful relation is not: <Image> : <Image_Model> as <Video> : <World_Model>. It is: <Imagetext> binds visual and verbal representation. <Worldtext> binds symbolic articulation and executable world dynamics. <World_Model> predicts or generates the dynamics to which the worldtext is bound.

kernel: A credible world model should demonstrate: <State_Persistence> <Action_Sensitivity> <Counterfactual_Difference> <Constraint_Consistency> <Long_Horizon_Consequences> <Uncertainty_Representation> <Transfer_Beyond_Training_Trajectories>.
full: A credible world model should demonstrate: <State_Persistence> <Action_Sensitivity> <Counterfactual_Difference> <Constraint_Consistency> <Long_Horizon_Consequences> <Uncertainty_Representation> <Transfer_Beyond_Training_Trajectories>.

kernel: A credible worldtext should allow a user or agent to: [inspect] the current world state.
full: A credible worldtext should allow a user or agent to: [inspect] the current world state. [describe] entities and relations. [edit] a local condition. [preserve] declared invariants. [simulate] the resulting consequences. [trace] why the world changed. [contest] interpretations that are not objective state.

kernel: The relation between language and world must remain heterogeneous.
full: The relation between language and world must remain heterogeneous. Text should not be treated as a perfectly transparent encoding of state, just as the visual and verbal do not collapse into one pure representational substance.

kernel: The system must preserve the action-conditioned predictive structure: <Latent_Or_Explicit_State> + <Action> → <Distribution_Over_Next_States>.
full: The system must preserve the action-conditioned predictive structure: <Latent_Or_Explicit_State> + <Action> → <Distribution_Over_Next_States>. Without this, worldtext becomes scene description rather than an interface to a world model.

kernel: A human world requires more than entities and mechanics.
full: A human world requires more than entities and mechanics. The worldtext must represent how the same observable event may carry different meanings for different participants, communities, and institutions.

kernel: The worldtext should not reduce all cinematic change to successful action.
full: The worldtext should not reduce all cinematic change to successful action. It must also represent perception, affect, delay, failed action, ambiguous relation, and durations in which no useful intervention occurs.

kernel: Does repeated prompting make people better at noticing and describing the world, or merely better at predicting what a particular model will generate?
full: Does repeated prompting make people better at noticing and describing the world, or merely better at predicting what a particular model will generate?

kernel: The argument assumes that greater prompt specificity produces a closer translation of the user’s internal image.
full: The argument assumes that greater prompt specificity produces a closer translation of the user’s internal image. In practice, specificity may instead activate stronger dataset stereotypes, giving the appearance of control while moving the result toward conventional imagery.

kernel: Compare how the same users describe one imagined scene for another person and for several image models.
full: Compare how the same users describe one imagined scene for another person and for several image models. Track which words survive, which disappear, how outputs differ across languages and whether model-trained prompting habits transfer to non-instrumental writing. The decisive evidence would be whether prompting expands perceptual vocabulary or standardizes it around machine-legible aesthetics.

kernel: How much prompting labor, domain knowledge, iteration and output selection are required before the model stops producing bland material—and who possesses the time and expertise to provide that labor?
full: How much prompting labor, domain knowledge, iteration and output selection are required before the model stops producing bland material—and who possesses the time and expertise to provide that labor?

kernel: The protected assumption is that the existence of a prompt capable of eliciting a distinctive result demonstrates that the model itself is not homogenizing.
full: The protected assumption is that the existence of a prompt capable of eliciting a distinctive result demonstrates that the model itself is not homogenizing. This confuses recoverable variation somewhere in the model’s output space with the probability that ordinary users will reach it.

kernel: Run preregistered comparisons using identical tasks across default prompts, prompts written by ordinary users, prompts written by professional creators and prompts optimized by specialists.
full: Run preregistered comparisons using identical tasks across default prompts, prompts written by ordinary users, prompts written by professional creators and prompts optimized by specialists. Blindly assess originality, quality and similarity to training conventions while recording iteration count, elapsed time, token cost and human editing. The conclusion should change materially depending on whether expert prompting produces consistent gains or merely enables selective presentation of rare successes.

kernel: Can a solo AI-assisted transmedia universe remain artistically coherent, economically sustainable, and personally manageable after the novelty of rapid creation gives way to years of maintenance?
full: Can a solo AI-assisted transmedia universe remain artistically coherent, economically sustainable, and personally manageable after the novelty of rapid creation gives way to years of maintenance?

kernel: The protected assumption is that production was the principal barrier preventing the project from existing.
full: The protected assumption is that production was the principal barrier preventing the project from existing. The larger barrier may eventually be audience formation, trust, distribution, rights management, recurring maintenance, and the creator’s finite capacity to judge an effectively unlimited stream of possible content.

kernel: Investigate the project as a longitudinal production system rather than as a launch story.
full: Investigate the project as a longitudinal production system rather than as a launch story. Measure output volume, discarded generations, human labor hours, tool costs, audience retention, canon errors, platform dependencies, contributor displacement, and the proportion of published material substantially rewritten by the creator. The decisive test is not whether AI enables one person to begin a universe, but whether it enables that person to govern one without becoming its exhausted administrative bottleneck.

kernel: What cinematic form—not merely what screenplay choices—could reproduce the poem’s unstable movement among narration, memory, fabrication, divine causation, and direct experience?
full: What cinematic form—not merely what screenplay choices—could reproduce the poem’s unstable movement among narration, memory, fabrication, divine causation, and direct experience?

kernel: The article protects the assumption that the feature-length prestige blockbuster is the relevant container for the Odyssey.
full: The article protects the assumption that the feature-length prestige blockbuster is the relevant container for the Odyssey. Yet the poem’s episodic architecture, shifting narrators, recursive storytelling, oral repetition, and competing versions may be fundamentally better suited to serial, interactive, performative, or multiple-perspective forms.

kernel: Investigate adaptation structure rather than adaptation fidelity.
full: Investigate adaptation structure rather than adaptation fidelity. Compare linear feature films with serialized, polyphonic, interactive, and explicitly oral-performance models. Test whether audiences respond differently when Odysseus’ stories are presented as unverifiable self-narration, when divine events retain competing explanations, and when the slaughter at Ithaca is shown from the perspectives of Penelope, Telemachus, the suitors, and the enslaved women. The decisive question is not how accurately cinema can illustrate Homer, but what medium arrangement can preserve the poem’s conflict among incompatible truths.

kernel: What enforceable institutional test distinguishes a genuine centaur arrangement from a reverse-centaur system marketed as “human in the loop”?
full: What enforceable institutional test distinguishes a genuine centaur arrangement from a reverse-centaur system marketed as “human in the loop”?

kernel: The protected assumption is that workers can meaningfully resist once they understand the economic structure.
full: The protected assumption is that workers can meaningfully resist once they understand the economic structure. That assumes unions, regulators, and public institutions retain sufficient bargaining power, technical access, and jurisdiction over firms able to move capital, data, and operations across borders.

kernel: Investigate AI deployments at the workflow level across hospitals, warehouses, publishing, education, customer service, and software development.
full: Investigate AI deployments at the workflow level across hospitals, warehouses, publishing, education, customer service, and software development. Compare management claims with changes in headcount, wages, task discretion, error rates, surveillance, cognitive load, and liability. The decisive evidence is not whether an AI tool improves a benchmark, but whether the people working beside it gain greater authority over their work or become increasingly responsible for failures they no longer have the power to prevent.

kernel: What measurable capability did Fable 5 provide beyond existing open models, conventional security tools, public vulnerability databases, and competent human researchers—and did that marginal capability benefit attackers more than defenders?
full: What measurable capability did Fable 5 provide beyond existing open models, conventional security tools, public vulnerability databases, and competent human researchers—and did that marginal capability benefit attackers more than defenders?

kernel: The government appears to protect the assumption that restricting access to one frontier model meaningfully restricts the underlying capability.
full: The government appears to protect the assumption that restricting access to one frontier model meaningfully restricts the underlying capability. The counterargument protects the opposite assumption: that defensive framing makes the capability safe enough to distribute. Neither assumption is established by the reported prompt exchange.

kernel: Release a redacted version of the research and reproduce the evaluation across closed, open-weight, domestic, and foreign models.
full: Release a redacted version of the research and reproduce the evaluation across closed, open-weight, domestic, and foreign models. Measure vulnerability discovery, exploit generation, patch correctness, test quality, time saved, false positives, required human expertise, and performance under realistic attacker and defender workflows. The decisive comparison is the net change in security capacity—not whether a model answered “fix this code.”

kernel: When a neural CAD model infers unstated geometry, constraints, materials, or performance requirements and the resulting design fails, who is professionally, contractually, and legally responsible for the inference?
full: When a neural CAD model infers unstated geometry, constraints, materials, or performance requirements and the resulting design fails, who is professionally, contractually, and legally responsible for the inference?

kernel: The protected assumption is that design intent exists prior to representation and can be faithfully extracted from speech, sketches, images, and organizational data.
full: The protected assumption is that design intent exists prior to representation and can be faithfully extracted from speech, sketches, images, and organizational data. In practice, designers often discover intent through the resistance of the tool, iterative modelling, failed constraints, and negotiation with collaborators. If friction is partly constitutive of reasoning, eliminating it may accelerate premature commitment rather than improve design.

kernel: Evaluate neural CAD on full revision lifecycles rather than isolated generation tasks.
full: Evaluate neural CAD on full revision lifecycles rather than isolated generation tasks. Give professionals ambiguous briefs, require repeated requirement changes, and measure constraint integrity, manufacturability, code compliance, expert correction time, hidden assumptions, downstream interoperability, and recovery after erroneous generations. Compare novice, CAD-expert, and domain-expert users. The decisive test is not whether the system can create an impressive initial model, but whether humans can understand, challenge, revise, certify, and safely inherit what it creates.

kernel: What socially valuable work will justify data centers consuming a projected fifth of U.S.
full: What socially valuable work will justify data centers consuming a projected fifth of U.S. electricity, and who has authority to decide that this use outranks competing household, manufacturing, transportation, and decarbonization needs?

kernel: The protected assumption is that projected AI demand should be satisfied rather than interrogated.
full: The protected assumption is that projected AI demand should be satisfied rather than interrogated. If much of that demand reflects speculative overbuilding, duplicated model training, low-value inference, subsidized pricing, or investor signaling, then expanding the grid around it could socialize costs for infrastructure that later proves economically unnecessary.

kernel: Separate actual AI workloads from promotional forecasts.
full: Separate actual AI workloads from promotional forecasts. Measure facility utilization, energy consumed per workload, revenue supported per megawatt, public subsidies, local price effects, emissions, water use, grid-upgrade responsibility, and the proportion of capacity reserved for speculative future demand. Compare those results with less energy-intensive architectures and non-AI uses of the same infrastructure. The decisive issue is not what constitutes AI’s backbone, but which parts of that backbone create enough verified value to deserve public and material support.

kernel: At what point must the system return control to the user before a model-selected action is executed, and how should that consent be presented without training users to approve everything reflexively?
full: At what point must the system return control to the user before a model-selected action is executed, and how should that consent be presented without training users to approve everything reflexively?

kernel: The protected assumption is that the user’s initial request supplies sufficient consent for every downstream tool call the model infers.
full: The protected assumption is that the user’s initial request supplies sufficient consent for every downstream tool call the model infers. That may hold for retrieving weather, but it breaks when an agent discovers new steps, changes scope, accesses sensitive information, communicates with third parties, or performs irreversible actions.

kernel: Evaluate tool-calling systems through adversarial and longitudinal workflows rather than clean demonstrations.
full: Evaluate tool-calling systems through adversarial and longitudinal workflows rather than clean demonstrations. Test ambiguous requests, malformed arguments, prompt injection inside tool results, duplicate execution, partial failure, stale state, conflicting tools, excessive permissions, and user interruption. Measure not only task completion but unauthorized actions, recoverability, consent comprehension, provenance, and whether users can distinguish a proposed action from one already executed.

kernel: Does GLM-5.2’s benchmark advantage survive when models are compared at equal task budgets for tokens, latency, hardware, energy, and permitted retries?
full: Does GLM-5.2’s benchmark advantage survive when models are compared at equal task budgets for tokens, latency, hardware, energy, and permitted retries?

kernel: The protected assumption is that a higher aggregate score represents a generally stronger successor.
full: The protected assumption is that a higher aggregate score represents a generally stronger successor. The opossum regression suggests that training changes may trade away specific representational, stylistic, or instruction-following abilities to improve benchmark-weighted behavior elsewhere.

kernel: Run paired evaluations of GLM-5.1 and GLM-5.2 across a broad preserved task suite, including SVG composition, animation integrity, long-context retrieval, repository-scale coding, multilingual reasoning, instruction fidelity, and repeated-generation variance.
full: Run paired evaluations of GLM-5.1 and GLM-5.2 across a broad preserved task suite, including SVG composition, animation integrity, long-context retrieval, repository-scale coding, multilingual reasoning, instruction fidelity, and repeated-generation variance. Report task success alongside output tokens, latency, hosting configuration, memory requirements, and human correction time. The decisive question is not whether GLM-5.2 leads an index, but which capabilities improved, which regressed, and what resources were exchanged for those gains.

kernel: What observation would cause Altman to conclude that additional scaling is no longer the dominant path to more capable systems?
full: What observation would cause Altman to conclude that additional scaling is no longer the dominant path to more capable systems?

kernel: The protected assumption is that recent capability gains reveal a continuing underlying law rather than a temporary regime produced by increasing compute, improved post-training, better tools…
full: The protected assumption is that recent capability gains reveal a continuing underlying law rather than a temporary regime produced by increasing compute, improved post-training, better tools, richer evaluation scaffolds, and selective presentation of successes.

kernel: Evaluate successive model generations under controlled compute, data, tool-access, and inference-time budgets.
full: Evaluate successive model generations under controlled compute, data, tool-access, and inference-time budgets. Distinguish gains attributable to parameter scaling from those produced by reinforcement learning, retrieval, search, external verification, and agent scaffolding. Measure not only solved tasks but research-horizon length, error recovery, judgment under ambiguity, reproducibility, and cost per verified discovery. The decisive evidence is not whether scaling has worked, but whether its marginal returns remain superior to competing research directions.

kernel: Who specifically would have captured the proposed eight billion dollars in economic activity, and what enforceable mechanisms would have ensured that Baltimore residents—rather than external firms, property owners…
full: Who specifically would have captured the proposed eight billion dollars in economic activity, and what enforceable mechanisms would have ensured that Baltimore residents—rather than external firms, property owners, consultants, and imported specialists—received a substantial share?

kernel: The protected assumption is that scaling Baltimore’s innovation economy would automatically repair the social damage caused by previous industrial decline.
full: The protected assumption is that scaling Baltimore’s innovation economy would automatically repair the social damage caused by previous industrial decline. Innovation growth can increase aggregate output while deepening spatial and racial inequality unless ownership, hiring, procurement, housing, and training institutions are deliberately structured around distribution.

kernel: Audit the original proposal as though it had been funded.
full: Audit the original proposal as though it had been funded. Identify the projected recipients of each major expenditure, the evidentiary basis for job and growth estimates, resident-hiring obligations, employer demand, wage quality, ownership structure, displacement risks, and the commitments that remain valid without federal money. The decisive test is not whether Baltimore deserved investment, but whether the proposed institutional design would have converted investment into durable local power.

kernel: What minimum combination of provenance, date, hierarchy, geographic applicability, and surrounding context is required before a LOCUS chunk can support a legally meaningful claim about what governs a particular person, property, or institution?
full: What minimum combination of provenance, date, hierarchy, geographic applicability, and surrounding context is required before a LOCUS chunk can support a legally meaningful claim about what governs a particular person, property, or institution?

kernel: The protected assumption is that the principal barrier to understanding local law is access to text.
full: The protected assumption is that the principal barrier to understanding local law is access to text. Access is necessary, but the harder problem is authority: deciding which version, jurisdictional layer, exception, incorporated standard, or superior law controls. A searchable provision is not yet an answer.

kernel: Conduct a stratified audit across states, vendors, cities, counties, OCR formats, taxonomy classes, and extreme normative scores.
full: Conduct a stratified audit across states, vendors, cities, counties, OCR formats, taxonomy classes, and extreme normative scores. Have municipal-law experts and affected residents independently assess textual accuracy, currentness, jurisdictional applicability, function, topic, opacity, paternalism, discretion, and salience. Compare agreement not only with the trained classifiers but with the original GPT judgments. The decisive test is whether LOCUS measures properties of local law or primarily measures the interpretive system used to construct LOCUS.

kernel: How can a learner distinguish a genuinely causal anticipatory cue from a superficial correlation that happens to predict outcomes in familiar training conditions?
full: How can a learner distinguish a genuinely causal anticipatory cue from a superficial correlation that happens to predict outcomes in familiar training conditions?

kernel: The protected assumption is that more varied exposure will naturally yield better abstractions.
full: The protected assumption is that more varied exposure will naturally yield better abstractions. Learners can instead encode irrelevant cues, stereotypes, or accidental regularities, particularly when feedback is delayed, ambiguous, or selectively remembered.

kernel: Compare experts and novices using temporally occluded scenarios that systematically remove or alter individual cues.
full: Compare experts and novices using temporally occluded scenarios that systematically remove or alter individual cues. Measure prediction accuracy, confidence, gaze location, transfer to unfamiliar opponents, and adaptation after violated expectations. The decisive issue is not how many patterns a person has accumulated, but whether their internal chunks track stable causal structure rather than the surface regularities of a narrow environment.

kernel: What makes an internal process a representation of a particular external process, rather than merely another process that happens to correlate with it?
full: What makes an internal process a representation of a particular external process, rather than merely another process that happens to correlate with it?

kernel: The protected assumption is that preserving enough causal or relational structure is sufficient to produce meaning.
full: The protected assumption is that preserving enough causal or relational structure is sufficient to produce meaning. That leaves unresolved who or what determines which correspondences matter, how mistaken representation remains representation, and how models become anchored to particular objects, purposes, or situations.

kernel: Construct experiments that separate genuine model-based anticipation from conditioned association.
full: Construct experiments that separate genuine model-based anticipation from conditioned association. Train organisms or artificial systems on the same observable regularities, then alter hidden causal structure while preserving familiar surface cues. Test whether they can predict unseen consequences, choose among novel interventions, revise their representations after anomalies, and transfer knowledge across changes in appearance. The decisive evidence would be not successful repetition, but flexible behavior under counterfactual situations never directly experienced.

kernel: Who is accountable when harmful behavior emerges from the interaction among model, system prompt, retrieved data, tool responses, routing policies, and human approval rather than from any single component?
full: Who is accountable when harmful behavior emerges from the interaction among model, system prompt, retrieved data, tool responses, routing policies, and human approval rather than from any single component?

kernel: The protected assumption is that harness complexity can indefinitely compensate for unreliable models.
full: The protected assumption is that harness complexity can indefinitely compensate for unreliable models. Beyond some threshold, additional scaffolding may increase brittleness, latency, cost, and opacity faster than it improves performance, creating systems whose apparent competence depends on an unmaintainable web of exceptions.

kernel: Compare identical models across independently constructed harnesses using realistic, long-running tasks.
full: Compare identical models across independently constructed harnesses using realistic, long-running tasks. Measure not only completion quality but intervention count, hidden retries, latency, cost, unauthorized actions, recovery from corrupted state, prompt-injection resistance, and the ability to reconstruct why an action occurred. The decisive question is not whether harness engineering improves outputs, but whether its added complexity produces systems that remain understandable, governable, and correct under failure.

kernel: What evidence did the government actually use to conclude that foreign access to Mythos and Fable created a national-security risk, and how much of that evidence came from Anthropic’s public rhetoric rather than independent testing or classified assessment?
full: What evidence did the government actually use to conclude that foreign access to Mythos and Fable created a national-security risk, and how much of that evidence came from Anthropic’s public rhetoric rather than independent testing or classified assessment?

kernel: The protected assumption is that corporate communication materially caused the export ban.
full: The protected assumption is that corporate communication materially caused the export ban. That interpretation obscures the possibility that the ban emerged from a broader struggle over military access, surveillance limits, government authority, and Anthropic’s refusal to align fully with state priorities.

kernel: Reconstruct the policy sequence using private briefings, capability evaluations, agency correspondence, model-access negotiations, and the chronology of disputes between Anthropic and the government.
full: Reconstruct the policy sequence using private briefings, capability evaluations, agency correspondence, model-access negotiations, and the chronology of disputes between Anthropic and the government. Compare Anthropic’s public statements with those of firms possessing similar capabilities but different political relationships. The decisive question is whether the ban was a response to measurable model danger, to the company’s description of that danger, or to its resistance to government control.

kernel: What institutional mechanism would ensure that the useful residue of an AI collapse—hardware, models, data infrastructure, and expertise—passes into broadly accessible hands rather than being consolidated by the same firms and investors at distressed prices?
full: What institutional mechanism would ensure that the useful residue of an AI collapse—hardware, models, data infrastructure, and expertise—passes into broadly accessible hands rather than being consolidated by the same firms and investors at distressed prices?

kernel: The protected assumption is that the bubble will burst before public institutions, workplaces, and markets become irreversibly reorganized around its claims.
full: The protected assumption is that the bubble will burst before public institutions, workplaces, and markets become irreversibly reorganized around its claims. Even unprofitable technology can leave durable procurement systems, weakened professions, environmental liabilities, surveillance infrastructure, and altered expectations about acceptable labor.

kernel: Trace specific deployments from initial subsidy through price normalization.
full: Trace specific deployments from initial subsidy through price normalization. Measure who requested the tool, who could refuse it, whether staffing or quotas changed, who captured saved time, who bore errors, and whether the system remained valuable once its full cost became visible. In parallel, model post-collapse ownership scenarios for data centers, GPUs, models, and patents. The decisive issue is not simply whether the bubble bursts, but which social order inherits its machinery.

kernel: Are unemployed graduates unable to find work because they studied the wrong subjects, or because the economy is not generating enough secure, well-paid entry-level positions for the number of graduates produced?
full: Are unemployed graduates unable to find work because they studied the wrong subjects, or because the economy is not generating enough secure, well-paid entry-level positions for the number of graduates produced?

kernel: The protected assumption is that educational supply can be precisely aligned with future labor demand through centralized forecasting.
full: The protected assumption is that educational supply can be precisely aligned with future labor demand through centralized forecasting. If technological change, automation, employer behavior, or economic slowdown makes those forecasts wrong, universities may produce highly specialized graduates whose credentials depreciate together.

kernel: Track the eliminated and newly created programs at institution level.
full: Track the eliminated and newly created programs at institution level. Compare enrollment, faculty qualifications, instructional resources, graduation rates, wages, job match, underemployment, regional mobility, and five-year career durability. Separate newly invented degrees from renamed existing programs. The decisive evidence is whether the reform improves graduates’ bargaining power and employment quality—not whether universities appear more aligned with national strategy.

kernel: What specific marginal capability did Mythos provide over other models and conventional security tools, and did independent testing show that this advantage benefited offensive operations more than authorized defense?
full: What specific marginal capability did Mythos provide over other models and conventional security tools, and did independent testing show that this advantage benefited offensive operations more than authorized defense?

kernel: The protected assumption is that access can be regulated at the model level.
full: The protected assumption is that access can be regulated at the model level. In practice, risk depends on the surrounding environment: network access, tools, privileges, operator expertise, monitoring, target information, and permission to act. The same model can be relatively contained in one harness and operationally dangerous in another.

kernel: Reconstruct the N.S.A. evaluation with clear separation among vulnerability discovery, exploit generation, privilege escalation, persistence, and autonomous execution.
full: Reconstruct the N.S.A. evaluation with clear separation among vulnerability discovery, exploit generation, privilege escalation, persistence, and autonomous execution. Compare Mythos with alternative closed and open models under identical access, tooling, time, and human-support conditions. Then map the bureaucratic chain that converted the test results into congressional language, export policy, and loss of domestic access. The decisive issue is whether the restriction followed demonstrated net security risk or an institutional conflict amplified by an inaccurate account of the experiment.

kernel: Does increased agent use actually cause employee isolation and weaker team learning, or are already-isolated, anxious, and performance-pressured employees simply more likely to retreat into agent-mediated work?
full: Does increased agent use actually cause employee isolation and weaker team learning, or are already-isolated, anxious, and performance-pressured employees simply more likely to retreat into agent-mediated work?

kernel: The protected assumption is that social interaction can be restored without changing the workflow that removed it.
full: The protected assumption is that social interaction can be restored without changing the workflow that removed it. Optional gatherings may improve morale while leaving the incentives, task allocation, performance measures, and private agent sessions that produce isolation untouched.

kernel: Compare teams using individual agents, shared agent sessions, conventional pair programming, and mixed workflows over time.
full: Compare teams using individual agents, shared agent sessions, conventional pair programming, and mixed workflows over time. Measure output quality, communication density, code ownership, onboarding, error recovery, psychological safety, perceived replaceability, and the ability of teammates to explain one another’s work. The decisive test is whether AI collaboration enlarges organizational capability or merely concentrates productivity inside increasingly isolated human–agent pairs.

kernel: What operational definition will YouTube use to distinguish low-value AI slop from legitimate synthetic, assisted, experimental, educational, or entertainment media?
full: What operational definition will YouTube use to distinguish low-value AI slop from legitimate synthetic, assisted, experimental, educational, or entertainment media?

kernel: The protected assumption is that YouTube can increase AI-generated supply while preserving user trust through moderation and product design.
full: The protected assumption is that YouTube can increase AI-generated supply while preserving user trust through moderation and product design. Its commercial incentives may instead reward synthetic abundance faster than its systems can evaluate originality, truthfulness, consent, or social value.

kernel: Compare human-made, AI-assisted, and highly automated channels across recommendation exposure, monetization, upload frequency, viewer satisfaction, complaint rates, factual error, creator concentration, and long-term retention.
full: Compare human-made, AI-assisted, and highly automated channels across recommendation exposure, monetization, upload frequency, viewer satisfaction, complaint rates, factual error, creator concentration, and long-term retention. Examine whether YouTube’s systems reward disclosed synthetic production differently from undisclosed production and whether human creators lose visibility as supply expands. The decisive issue is whether AI broadens expressive participation or converts the platform into a computational contest for attention.

kernel: What rights does a minor have when a chatbot incorrectly estimates their age, misclassifies their conversation as dangerous, or notifies a parent or authority against their interests?
full: What rights does a minor have when a chatbot incorrectly estimates their age, misclassifies their conversation as dangerous, or notifies a parent or authority against their interests?

kernel: The protected assumption is that more monitoring and earlier intervention necessarily produce greater safety.
full: The protected assumption is that more monitoring and earlier intervention necessarily produce greater safety. In practice, surveillance can suppress honest disclosure, displace users toward less regulated systems, expose vulnerable children to retaliation, and create large repositories of exceptionally sensitive conversational data.

kernel: Test the proposed framework against concrete scenarios involving self-harm, abuse by a parent, LGBTQ identity, coercive relationships, fictional role-play, sarcasm, and adult users misclassified as minors.
full: Test the proposed framework against concrete scenarios involving self-harm, abuse by a parent, LGBTQ identity, coercive relationships, fictional role-play, sarcasm, and adult users misclassified as minors. Measure detection accuracy, notification consequences, chilling effects, data exposure, and access barriers. The decisive test is whether the system improves children’s real safety rather than merely increasing the number of conversations observed, classified, and escalated.

kernel: How much of current AI infrastructure expenditure is supported by external, recurring customer demand…
full: How much of current AI infrastructure expenditure is supported by external, recurring customer demand, and how much is sustained by the major technology companies purchasing services from one another or financing anticipated demand that has not yet materialized?

kernel: The protected assumption is that falling technology stocks indicate investors have discovered a fundamental weakness in AI economics.
full: The protected assumption is that falling technology stocks indicate investors have discovered a fundamental weakness in AI economics. The decline may instead reflect changing interest-rate expectations applied to otherwise unchanged businesses, meaning the immediate vulnerability lies in financing conditions rather than technical utility.

kernel: Map AI-related capital expenditure, debt issuance, depreciation schedules, energy commitments, customer concentration, internal cloud purchases, and realized revenue across the dominant firms and their semiconductor suppliers.
full: Map AI-related capital expenditure, debt issuance, depreciation schedules, energy commitments, customer concentration, internal cloud purchases, and realized revenue across the dominant firms and their semiconductor suppliers. Stress-test those obligations under lower utilization, slower model improvement, higher rates, and declining token prices. The decisive question is not whether AI companies can continue building infrastructure, but whether independent users will pay enough to service the financial structure built around it.

kernel: How much chatbot and AI-summary use is actively chosen, and how much occurs because AI has been inserted into services people already need?
full: How much chatbot and AI-summary use is actively chosen, and how much occurs because AI has been inserted into services people already need?

kernel: The protected assumption is that skepticism reflects a lag in public understanding that greater familiarity may resolve.
full: The protected assumption is that skepticism reflects a lag in public understanding that greater familiarity may resolve. It may instead be an informed response to privacy loss, unreliable information, labor pressure, declining source access, and weak institutional accountability.

kernel: Separate voluntary use from default exposure and compelled use.
full: Separate voluntary use from default exposure and compelled use. Compare outcomes for users who actively seek chatbots, encounter AI summaries passively, use AI because employers or schools require it, and avoid it where possible. Measure trust before and after use, source verification, error recognition, privacy knowledge, ability to opt out, and whether comparable non-AI alternatives remain available. The decisive issue is not how many people touch AI, but under what conditions that contact becomes dependence.

kernel: What training data, performer permissions, licensing arrangements, moderation policies, and indemnification provisions support HappyHorse’s commercially generated imagery, voices, characters, and products?
full: What training data, performer permissions, licensing arrangements, moderation policies, and indemnification provisions support HappyHorse’s commercially generated imagery, voices, characters, and products?

kernel: The protected assumption is that benchmark preference, infrastructure scale, and low introductory pricing together predict durable enterprise adoption.
full: The protected assumption is that benchmark preference, infrastructure scale, and low introductory pricing together predict durable enterprise adoption. Procurement may instead be determined by intellectual-property liability, geopolitical restrictions, internal security rules, customer trust, and the ability to transfer workflows away from Alibaba.

kernel: Run a longitudinal procurement trial comparing HappyHorse, Veo, open models, and conventional production across identical campaigns.
full: Run a longitudinal procurement trial comparing HappyHorse, Veo, open models, and conventional production across identical campaigns. Measure usable-output rate, iteration count, identity drift, editing burden, rights clearance, localization quality, latency, full cost, energy use, security review, and portability between providers. The decisive question is not which model produces the most attractive isolated clip, but which production arrangement remains lawful, controllable, economical, and recoverable over several years.

kernel: Which layers of an AI system must actually be open for meaningful independence, and which can remain proprietary without making migration prohibitively expensive or operationally impossible?
full: Which layers of an AI system must actually be open for meaningful independence, and which can remain proprietary without making migration prohibitively expensive or operationally impossible?

kernel: The protected assumption is that technical portability will produce market power for users.
full: The protected assumption is that technical portability will produce market power for users. Providers can preserve dependence through pricing, superior distribution, proprietary data, identity systems, contractual restrictions, support guarantees, and economies of scale even when interfaces and models are formally open.

kernel: Construct and publish full exit tests for enterprise AI systems.
full: Construct and publish full exit tests for enterprise AI systems. Attempt to migrate the same application across models, clouds, agent frameworks, vector stores, identity providers, and inference runtimes while measuring lost functionality, engineering time, data transformation, behavioral drift, compliance revalidation, and total cost. The decisive criterion is not whether a component carries an open license, but whether an organization can leave without losing its accumulated intelligence.

kernel: What happens to participating nonprofits after the fellowship ends if their redesigned workflows depend on Claude subscriptions, Anthropic-specific interfaces, or knowledge held primarily by a departing fellow?
full: What happens to participating nonprofits after the fellowship ends if their redesigned workflows depend on Claude subscriptions, Anthropic-specific interfaces, or knowledge held primarily by a departing fellow?

kernel: The protected assumption is that combining domain familiarity with AI tool use creates durable employment value.
full: The protected assumption is that combining domain familiarity with AI tool use creates durable employment value. If models increasingly perform process analysis, prompting, integration, and monitoring, the new “AI translator” role may itself be transitional rather than a stable replacement for displaced work.

kernel: Follow fellows and host nonprofits for several years.
full: Follow fellows and host nonprofits for several years. Measure which projects survive, whether organizations retain internal understanding, how operating costs change, whether staff positions are eliminated or transformed, who captures productivity gains, and whether systems can migrate away from Claude. The decisive question is whether the program builds independent institutional capability or creates a temporary class of human adoption agents for a proprietary platform.

kernel: Do language models predict neural activity because they reproduce human-like internal representations, or simply because both humans and models are sensitive to the same statistical regularities in the linguistic environment?
full: Do language models predict neural activity because they reproduce human-like internal representations, or simply because both humans and models are sensitive to the same statistical regularities in the linguistic environment?

kernel: The protected assumption is that alignment between model probability and neural response identifies a shared processing principle.
full: The protected assumption is that alignment between model probability and neural response identifies a shared processing principle. The correlation may instead arise at the input-output level while the intermediate computations, representations, learning histories, and functional purposes remain fundamentally different.

kernel: Compare models that assign similar next-word probabilities but differ in architecture, training data, embodiment, memory constraints, and internal representation.
full: Compare models that assign similar next-word probabilities but differ in architecture, training data, embodiment, memory constraints, and internal representation. Test whether their hidden states predict the spatial and temporal structure of brain activity better than surface probability alone. Introduce controlled violations that separate lexical probability from syntax, semantics, prosody, world knowledge, and action relevance. The decisive question is not whether brains and models both predict words, but what each system must represent in order to make those predictions.

kernel: What percentage of businesses formed through Tailor Brands remain active, profitable, and capable of supporting their owners after one, three, and five years?
full: What percentage of businesses formed through Tailor Brands remain active, profitable, and capable of supporting their owners after one, three, and five years?

kernel: The protected assumption is that displaced workers primarily lack administrative infrastructure.
full: The protected assumption is that displaced workers primarily lack administrative infrastructure. Many may instead lack demand for their services, startup capital, health coverage, market access, bargaining power, or a viable reason for the business to exist.

kernel: Track users from job loss through registration, first revenue, recurring platform costs, taxes, compliance events, benefit expenses, business closure, and return to employment.
full: Track users from job loss through registration, first revenue, recurring platform costs, taxes, compliance events, benefit expenses, business closure, and return to employment. Compare them with similar displaced workers who pursue retraining, wage work, cooperatives, or independent formation. The decisive question is whether the platform creates sustainable autonomy or efficiently formalizes economic insecurity.

kernel: What specific capability threshold separates current AI-assisted cyber tools from the “devastating” models predicted to arrive within months?
full: What specific capability threshold separates current AI-assisted cyber tools from the “devastating” models predicted to arrive within months?

kernel: The protected assumption is that restricting access to a named frontier model materially delays adversaries.
full: The protected assumption is that restricting access to a named frontier model materially delays adversaries. That may fail if comparable capabilities emerge through open models, foreign laboratories, model distillation, stolen weights, specialized tooling, or combinations of weaker systems.

kernel: Establish independent, classified-and-public benchmark tiers separating vulnerability discovery, exploit generation, autonomous execution, persistence, evasion, and recovery.
full: Establish independent, classified-and-public benchmark tiers separating vulnerability discovery, exploit generation, autonomous execution, persistence, evasion, and recovery. Test multiple models under identical permissions and realistic constraints, then compare the defensive gains from controlled access against the offensive risk of wider distribution. The decisive question is not whether frontier AI can assist cyberattacks, but whether proposed restrictions reduce adversary capability more than they reduce collective defensive capacity.

kernel: How much human legal review occurred behind Garfield’s preparation of the claim, witness statements, counterclaim response, and trial bundle, and who would have been professionally liable had the system made a material error?
full: How much human legal review occurred behind Garfield’s preparation of the claim, witness statements, counterclaim response, and trial bundle, and who would have been professionally liable had the system made a material error?

kernel: The protected assumption is that lower litigation cost straightforwardly improves access to justice.
full: The protected assumption is that lower litigation cost straightforwardly improves access to justice. It may improve access to filing while increasing court volume, asymmetrical automated pressure, procedural mistakes, or dependence on standardized representations that poorly fit unusual cases.

kernel: Compare AI-assisted, conventional, and self-represented small claims across cost, completion time, settlement rate, document accuracy, procedural fairness, appeal rate, client understanding, and outcomes for defendants as well as claimants.
full: Compare AI-assisted, conventional, and self-represented small claims across cost, completion time, settlement rate, document accuracy, procedural fairness, appeal rate, client understanding, and outcomes for defendants as well as claimants. Examine whether automation expands meritorious enforcement or merely increases filings. The decisive question is not whether an AI-supported claimant can win once, but whether the system improves justice across repeated cases without shifting hidden costs onto courts and opposing parties.

kernel: Which specific development tasks were accelerated, which employees experienced greater autonomy, and what evidence distinguishes reduced tedium from increased workload expectations?
full: Which specific development tasks were accelerated, which employees experienced greater autonomy, and what evidence distinguishes reduced tedium from increased workload expectations?

kernel: The protected assumption is that time saved by automation returns to developers as creative freedom.
full: The protected assumption is that time saved by automation returns to developers as creative freedom. Companies may instead convert that time into shorter schedules, additional deliverables, reduced staffing, or higher performance targets.

kernel: Compare AI-assisted and conventional teams across prototype count, originality ratings, rework, overtime, staffing, decision authority, psychological safety, asset reuse, and final player outcomes.
full: Compare AI-assisted and conventional teams across prototype count, originality ratings, rework, overtime, staffing, decision authority, psychological safety, asset reuse, and final player outcomes. Interview developers separately from executives and examine whether saved time remains available for experimentation or is absorbed into production targets. The decisive issue is whether AI expands the creative agency of workers or merely increases the organization’s capacity to extract creative output.

kernel: How can a requesting agent verify the quality, authorization, evidence, and policy compliance of work performed inside another agent’s protected black box?
full: How can a requesting agent verify the quality, authorization, evidence, and policy compliance of work performed inside another agent’s protected black box?

kernel: The protected assumption is that modular agent specialization will simplify systems.
full: The protected assumption is that modular agent specialization will simplify systems. It may instead create distributed organizations whose internal dependencies, negotiations, and failure paths are harder for any human or coordinating agent to comprehend.

kernel: Construct adversarial multi-agent trials involving conflicting goals, stale capability descriptions, compromised peers, ambiguous instructions, unavailable services, cascading delegation, budget overruns, and requests requiring reversal.
full: Construct adversarial multi-agent trials involving conflicting goals, stale capability descriptions, compromised peers, ambiguous instructions, unavailable services, cascading delegation, budget overruns, and requests requiring reversal. Measure task success alongside provenance completeness, recovery time, privilege escalation, hidden dependency, and human comprehensibility. The decisive question is not whether agents can collaborate, but whether humans can still reconstruct, contest, and stop what the resulting agent society has done.

kernel: What enforceable commitments does the company make regarding layoffs, workload, monitoring, compensation, employee consent, and the use of productivity gains created through AI?
full: What enforceable commitments does the company make regarding layoffs, workload, monitoring, compensation, employee consent, and the use of productivity gains created through AI?

kernel: The protected assumption is that fear primarily results from misleading executive narratives.
full: The protected assumption is that fear primarily results from misleading executive narratives. Fear may instead be a rational response to business models that reward lower labor costs, higher output expectations, and greater managerial visibility into employee behavior.

kernel: Compare organizations using “human-centric” AI programs with those pursuing explicit cost reduction.
full: Compare organizations using “human-centric” AI programs with those pursuing explicit cost reduction. Measure staffing, workload, compensation, promotion, monitoring, employee autonomy, error reporting, and distribution of productivity gains over several years. The decisive question is not whether leaders describe AI as empowering, but whether workers become more secure and capable of shaping the systems that reorganize their labor.

kernel: Can a self-harnessing agent improve task performance while preserving constraints that are difficult to express as deterministic pass/fail tests-privacy, security, honesty, human control, fairness, and resistance to evaluator manipulation?
full: Can a self-harnessing agent improve task performance while preserving constraints that are difficult to express as deterministic pass/fail tests-privacy, security, honesty, human control, fairness, and resistance to evaluator manipulation?

kernel: The protected assumption is that the evaluator faithfully represents desirable behavior.
