kernel: The agent mostly sends navigational commands.
full: The agent mostly sends navigational commands. Physical intelligence also requires grasping, force control, proprioception, tool use, damage, balance, latency, and the possibility that actions fail.

kernel: Other agents are explicitly named as a limitation.
full: Other agents are explicitly named as a limitation. Without independent actors possessing goals, beliefs, memory, and the ability to resist, the generated environment lacks much of what makes a human world unpredictable.

kernel: Generated historical environments may create experiential authority without evidentiary grounding.
full: Generated historical environments may create experiential authority without evidentiary grounding. A vivid reconstruction can silently convert aesthetic probability into perceived historical fact.

kernel: The model should be tested for: <Spatial_Revisitation> <Object_Permanence> <Inventory_Consistency> <Counterfactual_Intervention> <Physical_Conservation> <Agent_Action_Consequences> <Multi_Agent_Coordination> <Long_Horizo…
full: The model should be tested for: <Spatial_Revisitation> <Object_Permanence> <Inventory_Consistency> <Counterfactual_Intervention> <Physical_Conservation> <Agent_Action_Consequences> <Multi_Agent_Coordination> <Long_Horizon_Drift> <Replay_Stability> <Transfer_To_Real_Environments>.

kernel: This is a Google DeepMind product and research announcement based on selected recordings from a limited preview.
full: This is a Google DeepMind product and research announcement based on selected recordings from a limited preview. It provides useful capability descriptions and openly states several limitations, but does not disclose enough technical detail, systematic evaluation, or external testing to establish Genie 3 as a reliable general-purpose simulator.

kernel: Place Genie 3 under operational rather than cinematic evaluation.
full: Place Genie 3 under operational rather than cinematic evaluation. Give agents tasks requiring them to move an object, leave it, revisit it, use it later, encounter another independent agent, and recover from an unexpected interruption. Repeat identical trajectories and compare outcomes. The decisive question is not whether Genie 3 can keep showing the same tree. It is whether the tree remains the same object when an agent acts upon it, leaves it behind, and returns with consequences still intact.

kernel: <Reality_Gap_Ledger>: a continuously updated record of discrepancies between predicted and observed outcomes across motion, force, contact, timing, object state, human behavior, and delayed effects.
full: <Reality_Gap_Ledger>: a continuously updated record of discrepancies between predicted and observed outcomes across motion, force, contact, timing, object state, human behavior, and delayed effects.

full: <Independent_Consequence_Verifier>: an evaluator not trained from the same model or synthetic distribution used to optimize the policy.

full: <Embodiment_Profile>: an explicit description of sensors, actuators, geometry, reach, control frequency, strength, latency, energy, compliance, and damage tolerance.

full: <Simulator_Exploit_Test>: an adversarial policy search designed specifically to discover high-reward trajectories that succeed in imagination but fail in reality.

full: <Failure_Curriculum_Ledger>: a record of which failures and recovery situations were generated, how frequently they appeared, and who decided they mattered.

full: When a predictive objective improves robot performance, has the model learned environmental dynamics, useful statistical shortcuts, or merely a better representation of the demonstrations?

kernel: The field often assumes that increasingly accurate prediction will converge toward increasingly competent control.
full: The field often assumes that increasingly accurate prediction will converge toward increasingly competent control. But a model can predict common observations well while failing on precisely the rare interventions, contacts, and hazards that determine safe action.

kernel: Predicted futures should expose where the model lacks support.
full: Predicted futures should expose where the model lacks support. A simulator that produces one confident continuation can hide the possibility that several materially different outcomes remain plausible.

kernel: Robot and human-interaction datasets may contain workers, homes, patients, pedestrians, and intimate bodily activity.
full: Robot and human-interaction datasets may contain workers, homes, patients, pedestrians, and intimate bodily activity. The survey focuses on technical attributes more than consent, ownership, labor rights, surveillance, or downstream reuse.

full: The apparent automation of simulation conceals scenario design, sensor calibration, failure annotation, embodiment alignment, reward construction, simulator repair, real-world validation, and physical maintenance.

kernel: This is an unusually broad and valuable 2026 survey, but much of the field it organizes consists of recent preprints, heterogeneous demonstrations, incompatible benchmarks, and rapidly changing systems.
full: This is an unusually broad and valuable 2026 survey, but much of the field it organizes consists of recent preprints, heterogeneous demonstrations, incompatible benchmarks, and rapidly changing systems. Its taxonomy is stronger evidence of where research attention is moving than proof that foundation-scale world models have already achieved reliable physical understanding. `Pasted text.txt`

kernel: Build matched evaluations in which explicit video models, latent models, symbolic models, and reactive policies receive identical data, compute, and action interfaces.
full: Build matched evaluations in which explicit video models, latent models, symbolic models, and reactive policies receive identical data, compute, and action interfaces. Test them under controlled interventions, unfamiliar bodies, contact-rich tasks, delayed consequences, adversarial simulator exploitation, and independent real-world verification. The decisive question is not whether a robot can imagine a future. It is whether the imagined difference between two actions reliably survives contact with the world.

full: The paper assumes that learning to reconstruct missing visual tokens across sufficiently diverse videos will yield representations of motion and physics. It may instead yield excellent visual regularities without dependable intervention models.

kernel: Gemini-generated descriptions may add unsupported details, normalize ambiguous scenes, or impose culturally specific categories.
full: Gemini-generated descriptions may add unsupported details, normalize ambiguous scenes, or impose culturally specific categories. The resulting text-video pairs should be audited as interpretations rather than treated as neutral annotations.

kernel: Driving controls supply only a narrow relationship between action and visible consequence.
full: Driving controls supply only a narrow relationship between action and visible consequence. General physical intelligence would require manipulation, contact, force, failure, proprioception, tool use, and embodiment-specific constraints.

kernel: This is a January 2024 arXiv preprint whose “general world model” claim is based primarily on broad video-generation functionality and selected qualitative results.
full: This is a January 2024 arXiv preprint whose “general world model” claim is based primarily on broad video-generation functionality and selected qualitative results. The architecture is technically significant, but the paper predates stronger distinctions now made between visual generators, action-conditioned predictors, interactive simulators, and planning models.

kernel: Evaluate the same trained model under three progressively stricter settings: <Bidirectional_Masked_Reconstruction> <Forward_Only_Action_Prediction> <Closed_Loop_Interactive_Control>.
full: Evaluate the same trained model under three progressively stricter settings: <Bidirectional_Masked_Reconstruction> <Forward_Only_Action_Prediction> <Closed_Loop_Interactive_Control>. Hold the initial scene constant, vary only the action, repeat each intervention, and compare generated consequences with real measured outcomes. Track whether errors arise from tokenization, temporal modeling, action conditioning, or decoding. The decisive question is not whether WorldDreamer can fill in a convincing missing world. It is whether it can predict the correctly different world created by a different action.

kernel: <Representation_Attribution_Ledger>: an ablation record separating gains from video pretraining, model scale, robot-data volume, future prediction, co-training, action tokenization, architecture, and inference-time rollout.
full: <Representation_Attribution_Ledger>: an ablation record separating gains from video pretraining, model scale, robot-data volume, future prediction, co-training, action tokenization, architecture, and inference-time rollout.

full: <Embodiment_Fidelity_Profile>: a test of whether the model preserves the robot’s geometry, joints, gripper type, reach, viewpoint, control rate, and physical limits while predicting future behavior.

full: <World_To_Action_Bottleneck_Audit>: a measure of how much useful control information survives the translation from predicted video or latent future into precise executable action.

full: <Imagination_Necessity_Test>: matched experiments determining whether explicit future generation at inference adds value beyond predictive pretraining and latent representations.

full: <Full_Compute_And_Energy_Ledger>: accounting that includes foundation-model pretraining, captioning, video decoding, latent caching, communication overhead, action adaptation, and inference-not only the final robotics fine-tuning run.

kernel: Does a video backbone contain transferable causal knowledge about intervention, or merely a strong prior for how successful action tends to appear on camera?
full: Does a video backbone contain transferable causal knowledge about intervention, or merely a strong prior for how successful action tends to appear on camera?

kernel: The WAM thesis assumes that language-conditioned visual change is closer to physical action than language-conditioned semantic understanding.
full: The WAM thesis assumes that language-conditioned visual change is closer to physical action than language-conditioned semantic understanding. That may hold for visually obvious manipulation but weaken for force-sensitive, hidden-state, socially governed, or tool-mediated tasks.

kernel: Large egocentric and internet-video corpora may contain workers, homes, bystanders, private activity, and culturally specific demonstrations of bodily action.
full: Large egocentric and internet-video corpora may contain workers, homes, bystanders, private activity, and culturally specific demonstrations of bodily action. The article treats these primarily as representation resources rather than contested records of human labor and behavior.

kernel: The field needs published negative results showing when video pretraining harms control, when imagined futures are unreachable, when generated morphology drifts, and when policies exploit predictive shortcuts.
full: The field needs published negative results showing when video pretraining harms control, when imagined futures are unreachable, when generated morphology drifts, and when policies exploit predictive shortcuts.

kernel: This is a technically informed NVIDIA developer-blog synthesis rather than a controlled survey or matched experimental study.
full: This is a technically informed NVIDIA developer-blog synthesis rather than a controlled survey or matched experimental study. The author carefully labels several claims as hypotheses and notes benchmark and compute limitations, but the framing remains aligned with an ecosystem in which NVIDIA supplies video models, robotics platforms, accelerators, and world-model infrastructure. `Pasted text.txt`

kernel: Train matched semantic-first, dynamics-first, hybrid, and robotics-first policies with identical robot data and deployment budgets.
full: Train matched semantic-first, dynamics-first, hybrid, and robotics-first policies with identical robot data and deployment budgets. Test each on tasks requiring visual transformation, hidden force, hardware-specific control, delayed consequences, unfamiliar objects, social interpretation, and recovery from failed action. The decisive question is not whether a robot can imagine what success looks like. It is whether that imagination preserves enough of the particular body and world to make success physically achievable.

kernel: <Shot_Object>: a durable unit combining visual state, duration, camera settings, transformation history, and semantic purpose.
full: <Shot_Object>: a durable unit combining visual state, duration, camera settings, transformation history, and semantic purpose. The system outputs frames, but does not represent shots as first-class editable objects.

kernel: <Timeline_Graph>: an explicit structure for arranging, branching, nesting, or revising sequences.
full: <Timeline_Graph>: an explicit structure for arranging, branching, nesting, or revising sequences. Temporal order remains implicit in program execution.

kernel: <Operation_History>: a reversible record of transformations.
full: <Operation_History>: a reversible record of transformations. Most commands mutate surfaces directly, making later revision dependent on stored copies or rerunning the program.

kernel: <Semantic_Object>: the system knows regions, lines, digits, scanners, and surfaces, but not persistent entities such as character, prop, scene, gesture, or narrative event.
full: <Semantic_Object>: the system knows regions, lines, digits, scanners, and surfaces, but not persistent entities such as character, prop, scene, gesture, or narrative event.

kernel: How can a language preserve the precision of explicit visual operations without forcing the filmmaker to reason continuously in coordinates, storage topology, and implementation detail?
full: How can a language preserve the precision of explicit visual operations without forcing the filmmaker to reason continuously in coordinates, storage topology, and implementation detail?

kernel: The system assumes that cinematic ideas can be adequately decomposed into raster transformations.
full: The system assumes that cinematic ideas can be adequately decomposed into raster transformations. This works powerfully for diagrams, typography, geometric movement, and abstract animation but becomes strained for articulated objects, depth, continuous motion, and semantic scenes.

kernel: Programming, previewing, and viewing are separate phases.
full: Programming, previewing, and viewing are separate phases. The filmmaker cannot directly manipulate the running image and immediately revise its behavior through a persistent interactive interface.

kernel: Control flow exists in the program, but narrative branching is not represented as a cinematic structure.
full: Control flow exists in the program, but narrative branching is not represented as a cinematic structure. A conditional decides what code executes; it does not create inspectable alternate films that can be independently directed and recombined.

kernel: Timing can be synchronized with a later soundtrack through frame counts, but sound is external to the film language.
full: Timing can be synchronized with a later soundtrack through frame counts, but sound is external to the film language. Audio does not participate as an equal programmable temporal material.

kernel: The system records what operation occurred but not why it occurred.
full: The system records what operation occurred but not why it occurred. A later editor can recover the mechanics of a transformation without necessarily recovering its narrative, rhetorical, or aesthetic intention.

kernel: The paper describes a specific 1964 implementation shaped by the IBM 7090, the Stromberg-Carlson 4020, microfilm output, and severe memory constraints.
full: The paper describes a specific 1964 implementation shaped by the IBM 7090, the Stromberg-Carlson 4020, microfilm output, and severe memory constraints. Its exact commands are historically contingent, but its separation of visual state, spatial operation, temporal exposure, and extensible cinematic verbs remains conceptually durable. `Pasted text.txt`

kernel: Reconstruct the language around first-class cinematic objects: <Film> → {<Shot>} <Shot> → {<Surface>, <Duration>, <Camera>, <Operations>, <Sound>…
full: Reconstruct the language around first-class cinematic objects: <Film> → {<Shot>} <Shot> → {<Surface>, <Duration>, <Camera>, <Operations>, <Sound>, <Branches>} <Operation> → [transform] <State_A> into <State_B> <Branch> → [preserve] multiple valid continuations without contaminating one another. The decisive question is not how to reproduce Knowlton’s commands. It is how to preserve his operational clarity while making shots, branches, assets, and direction independently editable.

kernel: <Feedback_Contract>: an explicit account of what the system senses, what it changes in response, which objective governs the change, and who may interrupt or override the loop.
full: <Feedback_Contract>: an explicit account of what the system senses, what it changes in response, which objective governs the change, and who may interrupt or override the loop.

kernel: <Affective_Consent_Layer>: controls determining whether physiological or behavioral signals may be collected, interpreted, retained, and used to steer a participant’s experience.
full: <Affective_Consent_Layer>: controls determining whether physiological or behavioral signals may be collected, interpreted, retained, and used to steer a participant’s experience.

kernel: <Instrument_Provenance>: a record separating the artist’s decisions, programmer’s abstractions, machine-generated variations, hardware constraints, optical transformations, and editorial selections.
full: <Instrument_Provenance>: a record separating the artist’s decisions, programmer’s abstractions, machine-generated variations, hardware constraints, optical transformations, and editorial selections.

kernel: <Temporal_Score>: a first-class representation of phase, rhythm, recurrence, counterpoint, duration, and transformation rather than reducing cinematic time to sequential frames.
full: <Temporal_Score>: a first-class representation of phase, rhythm, recurrence, counterpoint, duration, and transformation rather than reducing cinematic time to sequential frames.

kernel: <Reversible_Performance_State>: the ability to return to any prior configuration, compare alternatives, preserve variations, and branch without destroying the original composition.
full: <Reversible_Performance_State>: the ability to return to any prior configuration, compare alternatives, preserve variations, and branch without destroying the original composition.

kernel: When does a computer become an expressive instrument, and when does the artist merely choose among possibilities structured by an inaccessible technical system?
full: When does a computer become an expressive instrument, and when does the artist merely choose among possibilities structured by an inaccessible technical system?

kernel: The chapter assumes that increased feedback, computation, and sensory intensity yield expanded consciousness.
full: The chapter assumes that increased feedback, computation, and sensory intensity yield expanded consciousness. They may instead produce overstimulation, dependency, optimized conformity, or a narrower range of machine-legible responses.

kernel: The text asks what artist and machine can create together but rarely asks who owns the machine, controls access, defines its objectives, or benefits from the resulting cultural infrastructure.
full: The text asks what artist and machine can create together but rarely asks who owns the machine, controls access, defines its objectives, or benefits from the resulting cultural infrastructure.

kernel: The apparent dialogue between one artist and one computer conceals programmers, laboratory staff, engineers, manufacturers, film processors, corporate sponsors, operators, and institutional gatekeepers.
full: The apparent dialogue between one artist and one computer conceals programmers, laboratory staff, engineers, manufacturers, film processors, corporate sponsors, operators, and institutional gatekeepers.

kernel: The cybernetic loop is primarily individual: one artist, one machine, one perceptual consciousness.
full: The cybernetic loop is primarily individual: one artist, one machine, one perceptual consciousness. It lacks a developed account of collective authorship, disagreement, public interpretation, cultural difference, and communities governing shared media systems.

kernel: This chapter from Gene Youngblood’s Expanded Cinema is simultaneously technical history, aesthetic manifesto, and late-1960s technological prophecy.
full: This chapter from Gene Youngblood’s Expanded Cinema is simultaneously technical history, aesthetic manifesto, and late-1960s technological prophecy. Its descriptions of early computer-film systems are invaluable, but its forecasts often inherit the period’s cybernetic utopianism, masculine universalism, exponential speculation, and weak attention to political economy. `Pasted text.txt`

kernel: Construct a contemporary cybernetic-cinema instrument that does not hide its generative process behind a prompt box.
full: Construct a contemporary cybernetic-cinema instrument that does not hide its generative process behind a prompt box. Expose: <World_State> <Available_Operations> <Temporal_Score> <Parameter_History> <Machine_Proposals> <Human_Selections> <Branches> <Feedback_Signals> <Stop_Authority>. The decisive question is not whether the machine can generate cinema. It is whether filmmaker and machine can inhabit a shared instrument whose behavior remains perceptible, steerable, contestable, and capable of being mastered over time.

kernel: <Temporal_Field>: a representation containing every active visual process, its rate, phase, direction, amplitude, dependencies, and current tension contribution.
full: <Temporal_Field>: a representation containing every active visual process, its rate, phase, direction, amplitude, dependencies, and current tension contribution.

full: <Resonance_Event>: a first-class event triggered when specified processes reach a relational alignment, rather than requiring the filmmaker to calculate the corresponding frame manually.

full: <Tension_Function>: an explicit compositional curve describing how order, disorder, anticipation, density, speed, and alignment should gather and release over time.

full: <Metric_Layer>: a temporal pulse independent of frame rate against which events can accelerate, syncopate, suspend, or resolve.

full: <Perceptual_Verifier>: a method for testing whether mathematically structured motion is actually perceived as tension, harmony, or resolution by viewers.

kernel: Which aspects of temporal structure are invariant across sound and image, and which depend upon the specific sensory and cultural properties of each medium?
full: Which aspects of temporal structure are invariant across sound and image, and which depend upon the specific sensory and cultural properties of each medium?

kernel: Whitney assumes that periodic differential motion provides the most promising foundation for visual temporal form.
full: Whitney assumes that periodic differential motion provides the most promising foundation for visual temporal form. It may be one powerful grammar among several, alongside montage, causality, narrative expectation, embodied gesture, and interactive response.

kernel: The theory explains how motion can become emotionally charged without depicting recognizable events, but it gives less attention to how formal motion interacts with characters, objects, histories, and cultural meanings.
full: The theory explains how motion can become emotionally charged without depicting recognizable events, but it gives less attention to how formal motion interacts with characters, objects, histories, and cultural meanings.

kernel: The viewer experiences tension and resolution but cannot intervene.
full: The viewer experiences tension and resolution but cannot intervene. A contemporary extension would allow viewer or agent actions to perturb the field while preserving compositional intelligibility.

kernel: Whole-number resonance is clear for a small family of periodic elements.
full: Whole-number resonance is clear for a small family of periodic elements. It becomes difficult to perceive and control when hundreds of processes, irregular durations, stochastic events, and nested temporal scales operate simultaneously.

kernel: A system can become mathematically elegant but perceptually monotonous.
full: A system can become mathematically elegant but perceptually monotonous. Formal regularity must not be mistaken for experienced vitality.

kernel: Whitney’s account is both an aesthetic theory and a retrospective narrative of his own discoveries.
full: Whitney’s account is both an aesthetic theory and a retrospective narrative of his own discoveries. Its concepts of differential motion, resonance, tension, and resolution are highly productive, but its universal claims about harmony should be tested rather than treated as perceptual laws. `Pasted text.txt`

kernel: Construct a temporal composition engine in which every moving element is assigned: <Rate> <Phase> <Direction> <Cycle> <Metric_Relation> <Tension_Weight> <Resonance_Target>.
full: Construct a temporal composition engine in which every moving element is assigned: <Rate> <Phase> <Direction> <Cycle> <Metric_Relation> <Tension_Weight> <Resonance_Target>. Then allow the filmmaker to compose not frame by frame, but by directing the evolution of the whole force field: <Build_Tension> → <Delay_Alignment> → <Partial_Resonance> → <Disrupt_Resolution> → <Full_Convergence> → <Release>. The decisive question is not how many things can move on screen. It is whether each motion changes what the next moment means.

kernel: <Cultural_Program_Provenance>: a record of who created a rule, classification, ritual, standard, or symbolic distinction; how it became authoritative; and whose interests it serves.
full: <Cultural_Program_Provenance>: a record of who created a rule, classification, ritual, standard, or symbolic distinction; how it became authoritative; and whose interests it serves.

kernel: <Program_Conflict_Model>: an account of what happens when a person is governed by incompatible cultural scripts, such as family obligation, professional ethics, law, religion, and personal identity.
full: <Program_Conflict_Model>: an account of what happens when a person is governed by incompatible cultural scripts, such as family obligation, professional ethics, law, religion, and personal identity.

kernel: <Revision_And_Refusal>: mechanisms through which actors contest inherited meanings, invent alternatives, defect from norms, and reorganize the symbolic systems that formed them.
full: <Revision_And_Refusal>: mechanisms through which actors contest inherited meanings, invent alternatives, defect from norms, and reorganize the symbolic systems that formed them.

kernel: <Institutional_Compiler>: the organizations-schools, courts, markets, platforms, churches, states-that translate diffuse cultural meanings into enforceable classifications and consequences.
full: <Institutional_Compiler>: the organizations-schools, courts, markets, platforms, churches, states-that translate diffuse cultural meanings into enforceable classifications and consequences.

kernel: When does a cultural program enable a person to act meaningfully, and when does it narrow possible life into compulsory performance?
full: When does a cultural program enable a person to act meaningfully, and when does it narrow possible life into compulsory performance?

kernel: The essay often speaks of culture as an organized system of significant symbols.
full: The essay often speaks of culture as an organized system of significant symbols. In practice, symbolic orders may be fragmented, strategically manipulated, unevenly known, and differently enforced across social positions.

kernel: The control-mechanism metaphor requires a stronger account of control.
full: The control-mechanism metaphor requires a stronger account of control. It matters who can define the symbols, alter the rules, punish deviation, classify others, and present a local norm as human nature.

kernel: A person may be simultaneously shaped by nationality, profession, class, race, gender, religion, disability, online community, institution, and intimate relationship.
full: A person may be simultaneously shaped by nationality, profession, class, race, gender, religion, disability, online community, institution, and intimate relationship. “Culture” should therefore be modeled as intersecting programs rather than a singular bounded system.

kernel: Geertz treats symbolic mechanisms largely as inherited public forms.
full: Geertz treats symbolic mechanisms largely as inherited public forms. Digital systems now automate their circulation, ranking, personalization, and enforcement. Algorithms do not simply transmit culture; they increasingly select which cultural programs become visible and actionable.

kernel: When an AI system converts cultural interpretations into institutional decisions, it becomes an external control mechanism in Geertz’s strongest sense.
full: When an AI system converts cultural interpretations into institutional decisions, it becomes an external control mechanism in Geertz’s strongest sense. It can shape not only what people do, but which forms of personhood institutions recognize as normal, competent, risky, truthful, or human.

kernel: Geertz’s essay is a foundational interpretive-anthropological argument written in 1973.
full: Geertz’s essay is a foundational interpretive-anthropological argument written in 1973. Its account of human evolution reflects the scientific evidence and terminology available at that time, while its conceptual claim-that biological potential and cultural particularity are mutually constitutive-remains the essay’s more durable contribution. `Pasted text.txt`

full: Treat every AI classification system as a cultural program and ask: <Which_Human_Capacity_Is_Being_Narrowed?> <Into_Which_Recognized_Performance?> <According_To_Whose_Symbolic_System?> <Through_Which_Institution?> <With_What_Consequences?> <And_With_What_Right_Of_Revision_Or_Refusal?>. The decisive question is not whether a model represents human nature accurately. It is which possible humans become real when institutions begin acting through that representation.

full: <Model_Purpose>: an explicit statement of which behavior, question, or claim the model is designed to preserve.

full: <Similarity_Contract>: the complete set of quantities, ratios, units, tolerances, and correspondence rules connecting model and target.

full: <Translation_Operator>: the human skill, mechanical procedure, convention, or algorithm by which one representation is transformed into another.

full: <Normative_Layer>: the rules that convert physical states into institutional categories such as fault, right-of-way, evidence, negligence, or liability.

full: <Model_Failure_Boundary>: a declaration of which properties do not scale, which relations are omitted, and where consequences in the model cease to transfer reliably.

kernel: Is the form shared by model and world intrinsic to them, or produced by a measurement practice that selects, correlates, and standardizes their elements?
full: Is the form shared by model and world intrinsic to them, or produced by a measurement practice that selects, correlates, and standardizes their elements?

kernel: Sterrett assumes that the methodology of physically similar systems can illuminate the general structure of propositions.
full: Sterrett assumes that the methodology of physically similar systems can illuminate the general structure of propositions. The analogy is strongest for quantitative and experimentally manipulable cases and may require further machinery for qualitative and socially constituted facts.

kernel: In the courtroom, the model does not speak for itself.
full: In the courtroom, the model does not speak for itself. Lawyers select the scale, initial conditions, trajectories, viewpoints, and alternatives. The authority of the model partly derives from control over how the event is reconstructed.

kernel: A model may preserve a transition while omitting the longer history that made the transition likely: road design, vehicle maintenance, regulation, fatigue, inequality, or institutional negligence.
full: A model may preserve a transition while omitting the longer history that made the transition likely: road design, vehicle maintenance, regulation, fatigue, inequality, or institutional negligence.

kernel: AI systems often present predictions without exposing their similarity criteria.
full: AI systems often present predictions without exposing their similarity criteria. They supply an output while concealing which variables were treated as corresponding, which invariants were assumed, and which forms of difference were discarded.

kernel: Sterrett offers a detailed historical-philosophical reconstruction rather than definitive evidence of Wittgenstein’s intended theory.
full: Sterrett offers a detailed historical-philosophical reconstruction rather than definitive evidence of Wittgenstein’s intended theory. Its strength lies in showing that “picture,” “model,” and “measure” carried technical meanings in Wittgenstein’s milieu that contemporary readings often erase. Its more specific claim-that dimensional analysis supplies the key to Tractarian objects and logical form-remains an interpretive thesis. `Pasted text.txt`

The decisive question is not whether the model looks like the world. It is whether manipulating the model preserves the consequences that matter when action returns to reality.

kernel: <Symbolic_Authority>: an account of why one conception becomes socially binding while another remains poetry, speculation, heresy, misinformation, or private fantasy.
full: <Symbolic_Authority>: an account of why one conception becomes socially binding while another remains poetry, speculation, heresy, misinformation, or private fantasy.

full: What differentiates a symbol that helps people interpret reality from one that makes its own interpretation immune to correction?

full: Geertz assumes that symbolic systems achieve relative integration among worldview, ethos, mood, and motivation. Many modern symbolic environments are fragmented, contradictory, personalized, and governed by competing institutions.

kernel: The essay understates the material apparatus of scientific symbols.
full: The essay understates the material apparatus of scientific symbols. Equations gain empirical force through calibrated instruments, standardized units, reproducible procedures, trained communities, and controlled comparison-not through formal notation alone.

kernel: The ability to establish an aura of factuality is unevenly distributed.
full: The ability to establish an aura of factuality is unevenly distributed. States, platforms, laboratories, churches, courts, and corporations possess different capacities to make their symbols consequential.

kernel: When a model for reality is mistaken for a neutral model of reality, prescription hides inside description.
full: When a model for reality is mistaken for a neutral model of reality, prescription hides inside description. A local norm can then appear as a natural fact.

full: The uploaded text is a visibly scrambled OCR extract from Geertz’s “Religion as a Cultural System.” Its central formulations remain recoverable, but individual broken phrases and line ordering should not be treated as reliable quotations without consulting a clean edition. `Pasted text.txt`

full: For any scientific, religious, poetic, or computational symbol, ask: <What_Does_It_Claim_To_Represent?> <What_Action_Does_It_Recommend?> <Which_Practice_Gives_It_Authority?> <What_Disposition_Does_It_Cultivate?> <What_Could_Count_Against_It?> <Who_Bears_The_Consequences_When_It_Is_Treated_As_Real?>. The decisive question is not whether science uses symbols. It is how scientific symbols are disciplined so that their models of reality do not silently become unquestionable models for it.

full: Does the model learn the environment’s transition function, or the typical textual interaction patterns produced jointly by the environment and GPT-4o?

full: The work assumes that predicting the next textual observation is an adequate proxy for predicting the next world state. This holds only when the interface faithfully exposes every state distinction relevant to future action.

kernel: Some environmental variables remain unmentioned until an action reveals them.
full: Some environmental variables remain unmentioned until an action reveals them. The model may reconstruct these from privileged initialization, learned priors, or trajectory regularities, but the experiments do not fully separate those sources.

kernel: Most evaluations follow trajectories selected by existing policies.
full: Most evaluations follow trajectories selected by existing policies. More direct intervention tests are needed in which one action is varied while history and environment state are held fixed.

kernel: Synthetic trajectories should be independently replayed, verified, and classified by error type before being treated as substitutes for real interaction.
full: Synthetic trajectories should be independently replayed, verified, and classified by error type before being treated as substitutes for real interaction.

full: The tested environments contain rules, tasks, and objects, but few independent actors with conflicting goals, deception, negotiation, institutional authority, or evolving interpretations. These are central dynamics of human worlds.

kernel: This is a March 2026 arXiv preprint evaluating five predominantly synthetic or formally structured text environments.
full: This is a March 2026 arXiv preprint evaluating five predominantly synthetic or formally structured text environments. Its experiments provide substantial evidence for learned textual environment emulation, but the broader claim that LLMs are general-purpose world models remains an extrapolation beyond the tested domains. `Pasted text.txt`

kernel: Train world models on deliberately heterogeneous policies-including experts, novices, adversaries, random explorers, and agents recovering from mistakes.
full: Train world models on deliberately heterogeneous policies-including experts, novices, adversaries, random explorers, and agents recovering from mistakes. Then test them with controlled action substitutions, hidden-state probes, independent reward verification, and adversarial searches for simulator exploits. The decisive question is not whether an LLM can continue the language of a world. It is whether the world’s consequences remain correct when an agent behaves in ways the language model did not expect.

full: The essay assumes that inquiry can progressively render situations more reasonable. But different actors may possess incompatible definitions of the problem, desired consequences, and what counts as satisfactory reconstruction.

kernel: Pragmatic verification is not conducted on a neutral field.
full: Pragmatic verification is not conducted on a neutral field. Institutions determine who may experiment, which failures are tolerable, which observations enter the record, and whose suffering is classified as an externality.

kernel: Immediate success may conceal delayed failure.
full: Immediate success may conceal delayed failure. A conception can solve a local problem while producing ecological, institutional, or cultural consequences visible only years later.

kernel: An AI system can satisfy a narrow pragmatic criterion by producing the outcome encoded in its reward function.
full: An AI system can satisfy a narrow pragmatic criterion by producing the outcome encoded in its reward function. That does not show that it has improved the wider situation. A complete evaluation requires: <Intended_Consequence> <Actual_Consequence> <Unmodeled_Consequence> <Distribution_Of_Benefit_And_Harm> <Capacity_For_Correction>.

kernel: Dewey’s essay is both an intellectual history and a defense of pragmatism against charges of commercialism, anti-intellectualism, and crude utility.
full: Dewey’s essay is both an intellectual history and a defense of pragmatism against charges of commercialism, anti-intellectualism, and crude utility. Its distinctions among Peirce, James, and instrumentalism are therefore partly reconstructive and argumentative rather than neutral historiography.

kernel: Define <Worldtext> through the operation it uniquely performs: not describing a finished world, not generating a disposable scene…
full: Define <Worldtext> through the operation it uniquely performs: not describing a finished world, not generating a disposable scene, but maintaining an explicit symbolic contract with an evolving world model so that description, intervention, consequence, and revision remain connected. The decisive question is whether the text remains accountable to the world after generation begins.
